comment.body:
1) you have to have a profit target, (as you have a loss limit, have a profit limit too) it prevents you from being greedy, overtrading, gambling.. or simply from just getting lost in the trading meaningless and miss life. Very important. Growth matters, not sudden big gains. It's not a lottery ticket, it's a derivative contract.
2) again, you have to understand what lvls matters, and where can be potential rejection points, that can change the momentum. And so you take profit, set the sell order at or above breakeven, and so you ensures green trade

comment.body:
i don't like to DCA into a position more than 3 times. 
this week i only took 2 DCAs at max on the positions if it was necessary. i even said it. 
but then I average out of the position as well.
i always weight the last portion. 
so lets say: first leg is 40% of my daily money, then if market goes against me, my second leg is 60% (if i go for 3 DCAs, it is 30% 22% then 48%), and the last leg is always ATM
when i getting out, i take out 20% first, then 30% then 50%

comment.body:
if you want to do some homework, lets take one of the trade you found the best from me,
look back OD's historical chart, my post, my chart, my lvls for that day,
take a paper, and calculate how you would do it.
where would you enter, how would you deteremine the money you wanna work with, how you divide it, and how you take the position, and how you exit the position... and then you will get a PnL.

don't overcomplicate it... just a simple schematic calculation with prices. 
also keep in mind that how much portion you should use on that trade, taking your incomes and spending into account, to not risk tail-events in your home budget. (family, insurance, accident, hospital ,etc.)
